chunks.text: Behind the Olympics, there are countless stories the media does not show.
The stories of dedication carried by each athlete over many years.
Early in the morning, athletes gather at the training ground.
The one they compete against is often themselves of yesterday.
Frequent practice produces a tremendous result.
At the opening ceremony, thousands of athlete march in.
They raise their national flags and exchange greetings in harmony.
For that moment alone, every nation becomes one.
During competition, an athlete sometimes undergoes an injury.
Some keep running even while enduring pain.
On the back of one running desperately, there is something beyond mere victory or defeat.
Athletes who did not take a medal return home to their families.
Even in tears of disappointment, there is the resolve for the next attempt.
The true significance of the Olympics does not lie in the number of gold medals.
At the closing ceremony, athletes from every nation mingle as they enter.
Ethnic and religion do not matter.
In that moment, sport teaches us that it is a common language of humanity
